task.name: Karpathy Nitter Latest Five
task.goal: Open Andrej Karpathy's Nitter profile, extract the latest five timeline entries with detailed fields, and save the structured result as JSON.

expected_outputs.tweets: Latest tweet objects with ids, URLs, author info, publish label, content text, links, media, and reply/retweet flags.
expected_outputs.artifacts.result_path: Path to the persisted result.json artifact.
expected_outputs.artifacts.profile_state_path: Path to the persisted profile-state.json diagnostic artifact.

environment.agent_scope: caller-provided X_AGENT_ID or the default public scope
environment.profile_expectation: Uses the shared browser-cli daemon and currently succeeds when the daemon is running on the extension driver.
browser_assumptions: The Nitter profile page renders a public timeline as HTML with .timeline-item entries.
The task should avoid large snapshot or HTML payloads and instead extract compact structured data directly from the page.

steps: Open the public Nitter profile for Andrej Karpathy.
Wait for the page to settle and capture a compact page-state diagnostic object.
Run a compact in-page extraction over the first five .timeline-item nodes and normalize the result into JSON.
Persist profile-state.json and result.json in the task artifacts directory.
key_assertions: A valid run should produce exactly five extracted tweet objects.
The extraction should not rely on snapshot refs or full HTML capture.
If no tweets are extracted, result.json must fail explicitly instead of inventing tweet content.

retryable_steps: Retry later if Nitter itself is unavailable or returns a sparse profile page.
Increase wait_seconds slightly if the profile title loads before the timeline content.
alternate_paths: If the page structure changes, adjust the extraction query set around .timeline-item and the tweet-date/tweet-content selectors.
If browser-cli transport limits are raised later, snapshot or HTML artifacts can be reintroduced as optional diagnostics.
recovery_hints.stale_ref_strategy: This task intentionally avoids semantic refs because a full-page snapshot currently risks exceeding the daemon response size limit on large timelines.
known_wait_points: Initial profile render after opening https://nitter.net/karpathy.
anti_bot_recovery: If the profile page title resolves but the timeline count is zero, treat it as a source-side or rendering issue and fail explicitly.

failures.reason: The current environment produced an empty DOM for both the profile and RSS routes.
failures.signal: browser-cli html returned an empty HTML shell and page state showed body length 0.
failures.resolution: Do not fabricate data; mark the source as unavailable.

failures.reason: Full snapshot capture exceeded the current daemon line-based transport limit on the Nitter profile page.
failures.signal: Flow.snapshot() failed with 'Separator is not found, and chunk exceed the limit'.
failures.resolution: Converge the task to compact self-executing eval extraction instead of snapshot/html artifacts.

failures.reason: Arrow-function eval payloads returned null under the current extension driver implementation.
failures.signal: browser-cli eval '() => document.title' returned result=null while '(() => document.title)()' returned the expected string.
failures.resolution: Use self-executing JavaScript expressions in this task until extension eval semantics are aligned with Playwright.

semantic_ref_notes: This task currently avoids refs on purpose; the page is better handled as compact structured extraction.
anti_bot_notes: This target behaves differently between managed and extension drivers; extension mode currently renders the profile correctly.
output_interpretation_notes: result.json is authoritative only when ok=true.
The published_label field may be relative for very recent entries, because Nitter renders mixed absolute and relative labels.
